page: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-360-lssd is a memory-optimized server powered by AMD EPYC 9B14 processors using the x86_64 architecture. It features 360 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 180 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The system is equipped with 2880 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage is bundled, consisting of 12864 GB of local NVMe SSD capacity distributed across 32 drives. No GPUs are included in this configuration.

Benchmark results show top-tier performance across multiple domains. In multi-core CPU testing, the server achieves top 10% placement in stress-ng and Passmark CPU scores. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only large block speeds reaching 701.26 GB/sec and write speeds at 354.62 GB/sec, both ranking in the top 10%. Database capabilities are highlighted by top-tier performance in Passmark database operations and Redis SET operations, which reach 393.33 million operations per second. For specialized tasks, it demonstrates top-tier efficiency in software compilation with Clang, ray tracing, and LLM inference prompt processing across small, medium, and large models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this server represents a high-density resource profile. The integration of massive memory capacity alongside extensive local NVMe SSD storage reduces the need for external network-attached storage, optimizing resource density for data-intensive workloads. However, a potential tradeoff is the lack of hardware GPU acceleration, meaning parallel processing tasks like LLM text generation must rely entirely on the CPU, where text generation speeds range from average to strong rather than top-tier.

This server is designed for memory-intensive and storage-heavy enterprise workloads. It is highly suitable for large-scale in-memory databases, high-throughput database operations, massive software compilation pipelines, static web serving at scale, and CPU-based LLM prompt processing.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-360-lssd is a memory-optimized server featuring 360 dedicated vCPUs from AMD EPYC 9B14 processors on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 2880 GB of RAM, offering 8.0 GB of memory per core, and includes 12864 GB of local NVMe SSD storage across 32 drives, with no GPU accelerators. The server delivers top-tier performance in multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and database operations, as evidenced by top 10% scores in Passmark and Redis benchmarks. It also excels in software compilation and LLM prompt processing. This high-density hardware profile provides qualitative cost efficiency by bundling massive memory and local storage, though it lacks GPU acceleration for text generation. It is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, high-throughput transactional databases, and intensive data processing workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-360-lssd is a memory-optimized server with 360 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 2880 GB RAM, and 12864 GB local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier database and multi-core performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-360-lssd features 360 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 2880 GB RAM, and 12864 GB local NVMe SSD for high-performance database workloads.
tagline: A high-density, memory-optimized platform featuring massive local NVMe storage and top-tier multi-core processing for demanding enterprise database workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 360 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 2880 GB of RAM.
Bundles 12864 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage across 32 drives.
Delivers top-tier memory bandwidth and multi-core CPU benchmark performance.
Achieves top 10% performance in Redis and database operations.
Best suited for large-scale in-memory databases and heavy data processing.
